intro_text: p_l thinks r_c is, well, really, just — uh, just swell, and {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} {VERB/p_l/think/thinks} r_c might like {PRONOUN/p_l/object} too. {PRONOUN/p_l/subject/CAP}{VERB/p_l/'ve/'s} been building up {PRONOUN/p_l/poss} confidence, and {PRONOUN/p_l/subject}{VERB/p_l/'re/'s} going to ask r_c to spend some time together, just the two of them, this afternoon!
decline_text: Unfortunately, r_c is sent on an afternoon border patrol before p_l can say anything.

success_outcomes.text: r_c agrees to the walk, tail-tip twitching with excitement. It's a feeling p_l openly mirrors, ears forward, pelt prickling with anxiety in the best way as they pad through the forest together, the shifting dappled light beneath the canopy a green-tinted backdrop to the excitement of this budding dynamic to their relationship.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to took a romantic stroll in the greenleaf sun together.

success_outcomes.text: All of p_l's bravery is rewarded when r_c says yes! The pair head out for their romantic stroll, the buzz of excitement in their hearts merging with the buzz of life in the green-lit forest, shyly sharing glances between one another.

log.cats_from: cat_from and cat_to took a romantic stroll in the greenleaf sun together.

success_outcomes.text: s_c has always had a skilled tongue, but it's rarely ever felt this important to choose {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} words carefully. It's all worth it to hear r_c's 'yes.' Their stroll together is awkward with the new tension of acknowledged romance, but neither cat would change it for the world.

log.cats_from: s_c's social adeptness made {PRONOUN/s_c/poss} romantic greenleaf stroll with r_c all the better.

fail_outcomes.text: p_l awkwardly stutters {PRONOUN/p_l/poss} way through asking r_c out — and {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} {VERB/r_c/say/says} no. It's gentle, but it's a no. Tail tucked, p_l slinks off to be alone.

log.cats_from: p_l felt embarrassed when r_c rejected {PRONOUN/p_l/poss} offer of a romantic stroll.
r_c felt awkward when p_l asked {PRONOUN/r_c/object} out on a romantic stroll, and r_c rejected the offer.

intro_text: As the afternoon turns to evening and the patrol finally reaches the shade of one of the older trees in the territory, p_l asks the apprentices to gather 'round. High above the thrushes sing, and p_l prepares to tell the story of a unique bird of c_n's territory — one fondly recalled from kithood.
decline_text: p_l gets too distracted by the lovely birdsong to continue.

success_outcomes.text: Varied thrushes are sexually dimorphic, unlike most thrushes but like their cousins, the bluebirds and the robin. The males are dark gray and burnt orange, while the females and immatures are brown and dull orange. Above, they sing quiet but lovely beez and peez in the treetops of conifers.

log.cats_from: cat_from happily listened to cat_to's lesson on varied thrushes.
log.cats_to: cat_to enjoyed teaching cat_from about the varied thrush.

success_outcomes.text: Veeries, named after their calls, are shy cinnamon-and-white thrushes. They nest on the grounds of dense forests, which makes it easy to steal their nutrient-rich eggs—or, p_l adds, to watch the chicks grow up and fledge.

log.cats_from: cat_from happily listened to cat_to's lesson on veeries.
log.cats_to: cat_to enjoyed teaching cat_from about the veery.

success_outcomes.text: Gray-cheeked thrushes, as one might guess, are taupe-and-white birds with grayer cheeks, whose breasts are spotted with dark brown. From the treetops, they belt a lilting song. They nest in low down branch forks of trees such as willows, alders, spruces, birches, or firs.

log.cats_from: cat_from happily listened to cat_to's lesson on gray-cheeked thrushes.
log.cats_to: cat_to enjoyed teaching cat_from about the gray-cheeked thrush.

success_outcomes.text: Wood thrushes are recognized by their soft ee-oh-lays ending in a metallic trill, describes p_l, so beautiful that it almost tugs at one's heartstrings. Though rare, one might find these black speckled ginger-and-white birds foraging on the forest floor—providing an opportunity to catch one, or just a moment to observe nature.

log.cats_from: cat_from happily listened to cat_to's lesson on wood thrushes.
log.cats_to: cat_to enjoyed teaching cat_from about the wood thrush.

fail_outcomes.text: When r_c asks why they would even need to learn about birds that are too high up to catch, p_l scoffs. Can't {PRONOUN/r_c/subject} let {PRONOUN/p_l/object} reminisce?

log.cats_from: cat_from was annoyed that cat_to was more focused on whether or not thrushes could be hunted.

fail_outcomes.text: When p_l mentions that {PRONOUN/p_l/subject} will be teaching the patrol about thrushes, s_c butts in. {PRONOUN/s_c/subject/CAP}{VERB/s_c/'ve/'s} caught so many cinnamon-and-white thrushes before! When p_l informs the rest of the patrol that {PRONOUN/s_c/subject} {VERB/s_c/have/has} been catching ground-nesting veeries, not the types of thrushes that nest higher up, s_c feels too embarrassed to stay and learn with the group.

log.cats_from: cat_from lost respect for cat_to when {PRONOUN/cat_to/subject} acted like {PRONOUN/cat_to/subject} knew everything about thrushes.
